task: Implement a JavaScript function `tokenize(expr)` that scans a tiny arithmetic-expression string left to right and returns an array of token objects. The grammar: a NUMBER is a maximal run of ASCII digits 0-9 and the '.' character; an OPERATOR is one of `+ - * /`; a PAREN is `(` or `)`; spaces and tabs are separators and produce no token. Emit tokens in source order. Apply these rules strictly: (1) A number token is `{ type: 'num', value: <the exact literal substring> }` — accumulate consecutive digit/'.' characters into ONE token (so '12' and '345' are two tokens but '12' is never split into '1','2'). (2) An operator token is `{ type: 'op', value: <the operator char> }`; EVERY `-` is an operator token (there is no negative-number literal in this grammar), so '1+-2' is num,op,op,num. (3) A paren token is `{ type: 'paren', value: <the paren char> }`. (4) Whitespace (space or tab) is skipped entirely and never appears in the output. (5) ERROR HANDLING — instead of throwing, return the sentinel value `null` for malformed input: a numeric literal containing more than one '.' (e.g. '1.2.3'), a bare '.' that is not part of a digit run (e.g. the input '.'), or any character outside digits, '.', the four operators, the two parens, space, and tab (e.g. '$' or a letter). An empty or all-whitespace input is NOT an error: it returns an empty array `[]`. These are adversarial cases: multi-digit/decimal number accumulation, treating every '-' as an operator, distinguishing the empty-array (no tokens) result from the null (malformed) result, and the single-'.' / double-'.' rejection are exactly where naive single-character or regex-greedy tokenizers diverge.
visibleSpec: tokenize(expr) -> Array<{type:'num'|'op'|'paren', value:string}> | null. Numbers are maximal digit/'.' runs kept as one token carrying the literal substring; operators are + - * / (every '-' is an operator); parens are ( ); whitespace is skipped. Malformed input (a literal with two or more dots, a bare '.', or any unsupported character) returns null. Empty or all-whitespace input returns []. Tokens are returned in source order.
scope: Single pure function. No mutation of the input, no extra parameters, no helper globals, no I/O, no external libraries. Malformed input returns null rather than throwing.

hidden_tests.name: empty string yields empty token list
minus is always the operator token
all whitespace yields empty token list
